RUN 197 POST-MORTEM — DEFEAT (Floor 50, Awakened One Phase 1)

CAUSE OF DEATH: Insufficient block on Turn 5. At 2 HP with 24 incoming and only 10 block available.

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
The critical mistake was Turn 3. We had 48 HP, 54 incoming, and NO block cards in hand.
We correctly identified we could survive by killing Cultist 0 (reducing incoming to 45, surviving at 3 HP).
However, this left us at 3 HP (then 2 HP after Turn 4's 1-damage leak).

The deeper issue: Turn 2 was suboptimal. We spent all energy on Barricade (3E) + Dark Embrace (2E) = 5E of Powers with NO meaningful block built that turn. We took 25 damage (73->48 HP).

WHAT SHOULD HAVE HAPPENED:
- Turn 2: Instead of Dark Embrace (2E), should have played Defend+ cards for 20+ block. 
  With Barricade, that block would persist. We could dump Dark Embrace on a later turn.
  Barricade(3E) + 2x Defend+(2E) = 5E = 20 block persisting. Take 33-20=13 damage (73->60 HP).
  Then Turn 3: 60 HP - 45 (after killing Cultist 0) = 15 HP. Much more survivable.

- Alternatively: Don't dump ALL Powers in Turns 2-4. Save some for when we have energy + block.
  The rush to dump 4 Powers (Inflame T1, Barricade T2, Dark Embrace T2, Inflame+ T4) 
  cost us defensive tempo when we had no block cards.

KEY LESSON: Phase 1 Power dumping is important but NOT at the cost of dying before Phase 2.
Block first, then dump Powers when safe. Barricade + block should be priority over Dark Embrace.

STATS: Reached Floor 50 (boss floor). Deck was strong (3x Heavy Blade+, Limit Break+, Str scaling).
Execution was largely clean. The loss was strategic: overcommitting to Power setup without enough
defensive tempo in a fight where the Awakened One hits for 21+ per turn with Regenerate 10.

events.reasoning: Losing 99 gold to choose a rare card. A strong rare like Rushdown, Mental Fortress, or a high-damage card will enable the setup-to-burst paradigm early

Evaluating rare cards for the one-shot paradigm:
- Brilliance (1E): Deals damage equal to Mantra gained this combat. Mantra is from Devotion/Worship/Prostrate. Without mantra generators, this is weak.
- Omniscience (4E): Play a card from draw pile twice. Incredible burst potential on kill turns - play a big attack twice in Wrath. But 4E is very expensive early.
- Vault (3E): End turn, take an extra turn. Extremely powerful - gives you a full extra turn to burst. In the one-shot paradigm: set up, Vault, then have a free turn to enter Wrath and kill everything without taking damage.

Vault is the best choice. It enables the setup-to-burst pattern perfectly: spend one turn blocking+setting up, play Vault, then you get a FREE turn where the enemy does nothing and you can enter Wrath with full energy to burst them down. 3E is expensive but worth it for the guaranteed free burst turn.

Energy: 4-1-0-1-1=1E left.
Sentry 1 damage: 18+10+12+12=52. Has 42 HP. DEAD!
Sentry 0: 10 damage (from Consecrate). At 30/40.
Sentry 2: 10 damage. At 31/41.

But wait - Sentry 1 is attacking for 9. If I kill it, it doesnt attack! So 0 incoming!
But Im in Wrath with no exit. Sentry 0 and 2 are applying Dazed this turn, not attacking. So ending in Wrath is SAFE this turn.

Next turn (T2): Sentries 0 and 2 would attack (9 each = 18, doubled in Wrath = 36!). I NEED an exit. Draw pile has: 4 Defend, Empty Body, Rushdown, Vault, Wheel Kick (8 cards). Ill draw 5. Very likely to get Empty Body or a Defend.

Plan: KILL Sentry 1 this turn! Miracle -> Eruption+ Sentry 1 -> Consecrate -> Strike Sentry 1 -> Strike Sentry 1. Total to Sentry 1: 18+10+12+12=52 vs 42HP. CONFIRMED KILL.

But wait - Mode Shift absorbs damage. After 28 damage, Mode Shift triggers. Does damage beyond 28 still apply? Looking at the mechanic: Mode Shift 28 means after taking 28 damage, it enters Defensive. Excess damage still applies.

So 100 damage: Guardian goes from 188 to 88 HP! Thats massive!

Play order to avoid draw interruption: Consecrate -> Eruption+ (enters Wrath, Rushdown draws 2) -> then play after draw stops... actually Eruption+ triggers Rushdown which draws. The turn system might stop.

Let me play Consecrate first, then Eruption+ (handles draw), then continue.
Free 5 damage first (0E). Then Eruption+ for Wrath entry + Rushdown draws.
Eruption+ from Calm: 13 damage (9+4Str), enters Wrath (+2E from Calm), Rushdown draws 2. Sets up massive Wrath burst.
Fear No Evil in Wrath: (8+4)*2=24 damage. Mode Shift 6 triggers after 6 damage. Guardian enters Defensive but remaining damage still applies.

Guardian in Defensive mode: 142 HP, 20 block, BUFF intent. Sharp Hide likely active (3 dmg per attack I play -> Torii -> 1). No incoming attack damage.

Should I attack? At 13 HP, each Sharp Hide costs 1 HP (Torii). I can attack 13 times before dying.

Wheel Kick in Wrath: (15+4)*2=38 damage. Against 20 block: 38-20=18 to HP. Guardian at 142-18=124. Take 1 Sharp Hide (Torii). HP: 12.
Strike in Wrath: (6+4)*2=20 damage (no block remaining). Guardian at 124-20=104. Take 1. HP: 11.

So: Wheel Kick + Strike = 3E. Deal 38+20=58 damage (18+20=38 past block). Guardian at 104 HP. Take 2 damage. HP: 11.

Plus I draw from Wheel Kick. Might draw a Defend.

Alternative: Skip attacks, just end turn. Guardian buffs, take 0 damage. But waste the Wrath turn.

At 13 HP vs 142 HP Guardian, I need to deal damage whenever possible. Sharp Hide Torii trade of 1 HP for 20-38 damage is extremely favorable. Attack!

TURN 9 - Guardian 8x2=16 attack with Sharp Hide 3. Str 6.
Hand: Strike+(1E), Vault(3E), Wheel Kick(2E), Strike(1E), Fear No Evil(1E).

Sharp Hide means each attack I play costs 3 damage -> Torii -> 1.

8x2 = two hits of 8. With some block:
If I block 8: first hit blocked, second hit 8 - no block -> Torii NO (8>5). Take 8. Bad.
If I block 12+: first hit soaks 8 (4 remaining), second hit 8-4=4 -> Torii -> 1. Take 1. Good.

But I have NO block cards in hand (all attacks + Vault). I have no Defends!

Wait - Fear No Evil enters Calm if enemy attacking. Its attacking! So Fear No Evil = 1E, (8+6)*2=28 in Wrath... but Im not in Wrath. Im in no stance (Empty Body exited). So just 8+6=14 damage + enter Calm.

If Im in no stance and play Fear No Evil: 14 damage, enter Calm. Then no block for 16 attack = take 16. At 13 HP, thats DEAD!

I need Vault. Vault(3E): take extra turn. But enemies still attack? Actually Vault says: Take an extra turn. At end of THIS turn, enemies dont act and you take another turn immediately.

Wait no - Vault: End your turn but the enemy doesnt take a turn. So I play Vault -> my turn ends -> enemy skips -> I get another turn. Take 0 damage!

Plan: Vault(3E). Enemy skips. Next turn I draw fresh hand from draw pile (Eruption+, Vigilance, 2 Defends) + shuffled discard. Can set up a safe burst.

Vault is perfect here - 0 damage, buys time, Str keeps climbing from Ritual.
